target: data/sections.json part1 — insert evidence hierarchy roadmap paragraph between scorecard and section 1.1
insertion_point: After the closing of the scorecard div, before
replacement_html:
How This Review Is Organized

The dome model presents its own evidence hierarchy — a Steelman Evidence Ladder ranking its strongest claims. This review applies a complementary structure, organized by the type of argument rather than by topic, because argument type determines how hard a conclusion is to escape:

Tier 1 — Self-contradictions (Parts 2 and 2b): Arguments derived entirely from the dome's own stated parameters and equations. When a model's geometry predicts a Schumann frequency of 22–35 Hz but the observed value is 7.83 Hz, or when a local moon at 2,534 km produces one tidal spike instead of the observed two-bulge pattern, no external data is needed and no frame-of-reference objection applies. The dome's own mathematics refutes the dome. These are the hardest arguments to escape.

Tier 2 — Point-by-point evaluation (Part 3): Each of the dome's claimed wins assessed individually against published data and internal consistency. Many referenced observations are real — the verdicts address whether the dome's interpretation holds up, not whether the underlying data exists.

Tier 3 — Kill-shot binary tests and falsification tests (Parts 5 and 7): Structured tests where the dome and the globe make different, measurable predictions. These are powerful but rely on external datasets (satellite measurements, GPS timing, stellar parallax) that a determined defender could dispute as "measured from the wrong frame." The self-contradiction arguments in Tier 1 face no such objection.

This ordering is deliberate. A reader who trusts only the dome's own numbers and reads only Parts 2 and 2b will find the model already refutes itself. The remaining parts confirm and extend that conclusion with independent evidence from progressively broader domains.
holes_addressed: HOL-HIERARCHY: No organizing principle for parts 3-5 (ISS-774)
No explicit statement that self-contradiction > external falsification
No reader roadmap explaining evidence tiers
Kill-shots appear without framing on why they represent higher-tier evidence
new_evidence_added: Dome site Steelman Evidence Ladder acknowledgment (kernel of truth)
Explicit argument-type taxonomy (self-contradiction vs external falsification)
Reader guidance on which parts are escapable vs inescapable

anticipated_objections.objection: This roadmap oversimplifies — some Tier 3 arguments (Gaia parallax) are also very hard to escape
anticipated_objections.response: True, and the text acknowledges kill-shots are powerful. But the categorical distinction holds: self-contradiction uses only dome parameters, external falsification uses external data. The roadmap describes the logical structure, not a quality ranking within tiers.

anticipated_objections.objection: Adding a roadmap makes the review look defensive, like we need to explain ourselves
anticipated_objections.response: Scientific reviews routinely include methodology sections. The dome itself has a Steelman Evidence Ladder — acknowledging this and explaining our own structure shows intellectual rigor, not defensiveness.

anticipated_objections.objection: The roadmap front-loads the conclusion (dome refutes itself) before presenting evidence
anticipated_objections.response: The scorecard already states 0 survive scrutiny. The roadmap explains WHY we reach that conclusion by describing the evidence structure. This is standard scientific practice — abstracts precede methods.

kernel_of_truth: The dome model has its own evidence hierarchy (Steelman Evidence Ladder ranking Australia Scaffold, Eclipse Magnetic Ensemble, Tesla Disc Lock, and Single-Constant Unification). This shows genuine thought about evidence structure. Our roadmap acknowledges this sophistication while explaining why our own hierarchy is organized by argument type rather than by topic — because the TYPE of argument (self-contradiction vs external data) determines how escapable the conclusion is.
